senses.definition: to think hard or make a great effort to remember or solve something

examples.x: I've been racking my brain all day trying to remember where I put my car keys.
We've been racking our brains trying to figure out the answer to this tricky crossword puzzle clue.
He's been racking his brain, trying to recall the name of the actor in that movie.

senses.definition: to make one feel a sense of familiarity or help one remember something

examples.x: I mentioned his name, but it didn't seem to ring a bell with her.
I mentioned the movie title to him, and it immediately rang a bell, triggering his memory of watching it years ago.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk ɐ stɹˈoʊl tɹˈɪp wˈɔːk dˌaʊn mˈɛmɚɹi lˈeɪn

senses.definition: to talk or write about one's enjoyable previous experiences

examples.x: As we looked through the old photo album, we couldn't help but take a stroll down memory lane.
At the reunion, we all took a walk down memory lane, sharing stories from our school days.
As we flipped through old photo albums, we took a stroll down memory lane, reminiscing about our childhood adventures.
During the family reunion, we decided to take a trip down memory lane and revisit the park where we used to play as kids.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv ɐ mˈɛmɚɹi lˈaɪk ɐn ˈɛlɪfənt

senses.definition: to easily be able to remember things and rarely forget them

examples.x: My grandfather has a memory like an elephant; he can remember details from his childhood that I can't even recall from last week.
The detective had a memory like an elephant, and he could recall every detail of the crime scene.
The teacher had a memory like an elephant, and she could remember the names of all her students, even those she had only had for a few weeks.
The author had a memory like an elephant, and she could quote from her books verbatim.
The historian had a memory like an elephant, and he could recall dates, names, and events with incredible accuracy.

senses.definition: the unexpected appearance or remebrance of a person or thing that evokes nostalgic feelings within one

examples.x: What a blast from the past!
This yearbook picture is a blast from the past.
That nickname is a blast from the past.
Watching that movie again was a real blast from the past.
Seeing my childhood toy was a delightful blast from the past.

senses.definition: a person's ability to visualize things that could happen

examples.x: Even though she had never been to Paris, she could vividly imagine the Eiffel Tower and the bustling streets in her mind's eye.
As the author described the enchanted forest in the book, readers could visualize the lush trees and magical creatures in their mind's eye.
Closing his eyes, the artist could see the colors and shapes he wanted to paint in his mind's eye before even touching the canvas.
The architect envisioned the grandeur of the future building project in her mind's eye, visualizing every detail of its design and structure.
Despite the passing years, he could still picture his childhood home with perfect clarity in his mind's eye, recalling every corner and decoration.

senses.definition: to make one think of something one saw or heard before

examples.x: His new song has a familiar ring to it; it reminds me of a classic rock anthem.
The story of their unexpected reunion had a familiar rhythm, like something out of a movie.
The way he spoke had a familiar ring, as if I'd heard those words before.

senses.definition: to help someone remember something they forgot

examples.x: I couldn't remember where I put my keys, but retracing my steps jogged my memory, and I found them on the kitchen counter.
Her name is on the tip of my tongue; I just need a little something to jog my memory.
Reading through his notes helped jog his memory before the exam.
She tried to jog his memory by describing the event in detail.

senses.definition: used to refer to  events or experiences that have happened within the lifetime of people who are currently alive, particularly recently

examples.x: The last time our town experienced a storm of this magnitude was within living memory.
The economic changes in the country have transformed it beyond recognition within living memory.
This is the most significant political shift we've seen in living memory.
The oldest members of the community can recall stories of how things were in living memory.

senses.definition: (of a memory) to still be present in one's mind, particularly when one does not want to remember it

examples.x: The haunting image of that abandoned house has been ringing in her ears ever since she saw it.
Even years after the accident, the guilt continued to ring in his ears.

senses.definition: to bring up past painful or unresolved experiences, often leading to emotional distress or discomfort

examples.x: Bringing up their past breakup really reopened old wounds, and they both ended up upset.
Discussing the family's financial struggles only serves to open old wounds, so it's best to focus on the present.
We should avoid talking about the accident; it will only reopen old wounds for those who were involved.

senses.definition: as a way to remember or honor past experiences or relationships

examples.x: Let's have lunch at our favorite childhood diner for old times' sake.
We should watch that movie again, just for old times' sake.
Then one day, just for old times' sake, I paid a visit to Winston Street.

word_list.pronunciation: ɪf wˈʌnz mˈɛmɚɹi sˈɜːv ˌɛsbˈiː ɹˈaɪt ɔːɹ

senses.definition: used when one is trying to remember something, but is not sure whether one can remember it correctly

examples.x: I think the meeting is at 2:00 PM, if my memory serves me right.
If my memory serves me, we met her at a conference last year.
